channel.title: Wellness, Actually — Episode Articles
channel.description: Evidence-based health articles by F. Perry Wilson, MD MSCE, drawn from the Wellness, Actually podcast.

item.title: Does hormone replacement therapy actually work? What the evidence says in 2026
item.description: A nephrologist breaks down hormone replacement therapy: what the Women's Health Initiative really showed, who benefits, and what HRT can and can't do.

item.title: Do continuous glucose monitors actually work for people without diabetes?
item.description: A nephrologist breaks down whether continuous glucose monitors help non-diabetics, what the evidence shows on spikes, and when a CGM is worth $50.

item.title: How much protein do you actually need?
item.description: A Yale nephrologist breaks down the evidence on protein intake: how much you need, what it does for muscle and weight loss, and when more is too much.

item.title: Does creatine actually work? What the evidence says about muscle, memory, and your kidneys
item.description: Creatine is everywhere right now. Here's what the randomized trials actually show about muscle gains, brain benefits, kidney safety, and hair loss.

item.title: Do Stem Cell Injections Actually Work? What the Evidence Says About Regenerative Medicine Clinics
item.description: Regenerative medicine clinics sell stem cell injections for knees, wrinkles, and more. Here's what the data actually shows, and what you're really getting.

item.title: Are Microplastics Actually Harming Your Health?
item.description: Microplastics are in our food, our dust, and apparently our brains. But does the evidence actually show they're hurting us? A doctor breaks down what we know.

item.title: Does red light therapy actually work? What the evidence shows
item.description: Red light therapy is sold for wrinkles, hair loss, muscle recovery, and mood. Here's what the evidence actually shows, and where the claims fall apart.

item.title: GLP-1s for Weight Loss: What the Evidence Actually Shows
item.description: A nephrologist's honest look at GLP-1 drugs like Ozempic and Zepbound: weight loss, muscle loss, weight regain, side effects, and what we still don't know.

item.title: Do Cold Plunges and Saunas Actually Work? What the Evidence Shows
item.description: A Yale physician breaks down the real evidence on cold plunges and saunas, from athletic recovery to cardiovascular health, detox claims, and safety.

item.title: Do peptide injections like BPC 157 actually work?
item.description: Peptides like BPC 157 and the "Wolverine stack" are everywhere on social media. Here's what the evidence actually shows, and where the real risks are.

item.title: Are sperm counts really declining, and should you care?
item.description: Sperm counts have dropped over the past 50 years, but the cause isn't cell phones, soy, or laptops. Here's what the evidence actually says.
